mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor market conditions trigger wage-push inflation expectations, prompting the Federal Reserve to signal tighter policy, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall indicates supply-side bottlenecks constraining demand-driven growth. This mismatch elevates input cost pressures and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 55% of historical instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP growth below 1.5% signals a deceleration in aggregate demand that erodes corporate earnings momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 50% of triggered episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising inflation, higher nominal rates increase the discount rate applied to long-duration equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism only holds when inflation is accelerating, not when it is stable.
failure_condition: The negative return prediction fails during high-inflation periods, or CPI acceleration correlates positively with SPX forward returns in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in positive payroll growth months do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit outstanding expands rapidly alongside a rising federal funds rate, the combination signals unsustainable debt-fueled demand facing higher financing costs. This mismatch triggers margin compression and default risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative returns in at least 55% of historical instances, or the joint signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side contraction that, when combined with sticky prices, forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative during triggered episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
